California

Practices Commission
February 7, 
■99■
Mr. Phi■■ip E. Pau■e
P.0. Box 9425
San Bernardino′  CA 92427
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. f-91-056
Dear Mr. Paule:
In response to your letter dated January 23, 1991, in which
you seek advice concerning the use of campaign funds under the
Political Reform Act (the trActtr),4 we are unable to provide you
with the assistance you seek, and write onl-y after several
attenpts to reach you by telephone were unsuccessful .
Regrulation 18329 (copy enclosed) governs the process by which
the Conmission provides advice. Among other things, the
Conrnission does not provide advice in the absence of sufficient
facts (Regulation 18329 (b) (8) (c) , 1832e (c) (4) (F) ) , or when the
advice request is hypotheticaL (Regulation 18329 (b) (D) ,
18329(c) (4) (D) ), or when the request concerns another person's
duties under the Act, and the requestor is not authorized to seek
the advice (Regulation 1832e (b) (8) (B) , 1832e (c) (4) (c) ) .
It is not clear whether you are seeking advice for yourself
or on behal,f of another person. It is also not clear whether or
not your question concerns an actual sj.tuation. Therefore, there
are insufficient facts to pernit a response to your inguiry. 
If
you wish to contact this office and provide the additional
information necessary, we will be happy to provide you with an
appropriate response.

California Code of Regulations.

I am sorry that at this time we cannot provide yOu with the
guidance you requested.  If you have any questions regarding this

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought advice concerning the use of campaign funds under the Political Reform Act, but the specific nature and context of the question are unclear.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because there were insufficient facts, it was unclear whether the request was hypothetical or actual, and it was unclear whether the requestor was seeking advice for themselves or on behalf of another person.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter, not a full advice letter. The document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. The FPPC declines to advise based on Regulation 18329 requirements regarding factual sufficiency, hypothetical questions, and requestor authorization. Document contains OCR errors (redacted/corrupted characters) but core meaning is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requestor sought advice concerning the use of campaign funds under the Political Reform Act, but the specific nature and context of the question are unclear.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide advice because there were insufficient facts, it was unclear whether the request was hypothetical or actual, and it was unclear whether the requestor was seeking advice for themselves or on behalf of another person.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide informal advice on campaign fund usage under the Political Reform Act due to insufficient facts and ambiguities regarding the requestor's standing and whether the inquiry concerned an actual situation.
